name: Tradier
description_short: API-first brokerage platform designed for developers and fintech companies, offering commission-free equity trades and a robust trading API.
description_long:
Tradier is an API-first brokerage that puts developer access at the center of its offering. Unlike traditional brokers that add APIs as an afterthought, Tradier was built from the ground up as a brokerage-as-a-service platform, making it the go-to choice for fintech developers, algo traders, and companies building trading applications.

The Tradier API provides RESTful endpoints for account management, order execution, market data, and options chains. The documentation is thorough and developer-friendly, with SDKs available for multiple programming languages. Commission-free equity trading and competitive options pricing ($0.35/contract on the subscription plan) make it cost-effective for active traders and applications processing high volumes.

Based in Charlotte, North Carolina with 50-100 employees, Tradier also offers a traditional trading platform for non-developers. However, its primary value proposition is the API infrastructure. Third-party platforms like TradingView and other charting tools can connect through Tradier for execution, giving developers and traders flexibility in how they build their trading workflows.
pros: Best-in-class trading API with thorough developer documentation and SDKs
Commission-free equity trades with competitive $0.35/contract options pricing
Third-party platform integrations including TradingView for execution
Paper trading sandbox for testing strategies and API integrations
cons: Stocks and options only — no futures, forex, or crypto support
Trading platform itself is basic compared to Schwab or Fidelity
Smaller broker with less brand recognition than major brokerages
No proprietary research or analysis tools — purely infrastructure-focused
